content.text: Code Review Checklist for Claude Integrations

General code review practices — readable names, DRY code, clear tests — apply to Claude integrations just as they do to any other code. This checklist captures the Claude-specific concerns that a general checklist misses: injection attack surface in prompt construction, correct exception handling for the Anthropic SDK's error hierarchy, token usage observability, and PII handling in both the prompt assembly path and the response processing path. Reviewers who are not Claude experts should work through this checklist explicitly rather than relying on intuition.
A reviewer who has never written a Claude integration will miss injection risks, incorrect retry logic, and silent truncation. This checklist makes those concerns explicit and checkable.

attrs.alt: Six Code Review Dimensions for Claude Integrations

content.text: AsyncAnthropic is used in async contexts.

If the code runs inside an async framework (FastAPI, aiohttp, asyncio workers), it must use AsyncAnthropic, not the synchronous Anthropic client. A sync client in an async context blocks the event loop.
The client is a module-level singleton.
The client must not be instantiated inside a request handler, loop body, or per-call function. Look for
inside a function body — that is a bug.
max_retries and timeout are set at creation.
The client must be created with at least
and an explicit timeout. The SDK default of 2 retries is too low for production workloads.
API key is not passed as a literal.
argument in a client constructor call is a finding. The SDK reads
Model ID comes from a config constant.
call is a finding. Model IDs must come from a central

# FINDINGS — what to flag in review:
client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key="sk-ant-...")    # hardcoded key
client = anthropic.Anthropic()                             # sync client in async service
client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()                        # missing retries/timeout

response = await client.messages.create(
    model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",                     # hardcoded model string
    ...
)

# CORRECT — what it should look like:
# client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(max_retries=3, timeout=60.0)  # singleton at module level
# response = await client.messages.create(model=MODEL.default, ...)
raises BadRequestError at runtime. This is a bug, not a warning.
max_tokens is a named constant, not a magic number.
are findings. Each use case should have a named constant (
System prompt is a constant or template, not an f-string.
An f-string building the system prompt inline with user data is an injection risk finding (see Dimension 2).
User-supplied text only appears in the message list or in delimited section-5 blocks.
Review every variable interpolation into the system prompt. If user data (name, query, uploaded file content) reaches sections 1–3 of the system prompt without delimiters, it is an injection finding.
Namespaced delimiters are used for injected context.
or equivalent namespaced tags wrapping dynamic data. Generic tags like
are findings — a user can close and reopen them.
The system prompt uses the canonical safety baseline.
Check the version comment in the system prompt constant against the latest version in
. A missing or outdated baseline is a finding.
The prompt template passes the CI injection test.
If the PR does not show a passing injection test in CI, flag it. For new prompts, the injection test must be added before approval.

# FINDINGS:
system = f"You are an assistant for {user_name}."           # user data in section 1
system = f"Context: {user_query}\n\n{BASE_SYSTEM}"         # user data before safety baseline

# CORRECT:
SYSTEM = """You are the invoice assistant for Acme.\n...(safety baseline)..."""

def build_system_with_context(account_data: str) -> str:
    return SYSTEM + f"""\n<acme:context>\n{account_data}\n</acme:context>"""
    # account_data is server-retrieved, not user-supplied
    # user input goes in the messages list only
RateLimitError and InternalServerError (529) are handled separately from 4xx.
that retries all errors is a finding. Retrying a 422 UnprocessableEntityError causes infinite loops.
Review any retry loop: does it check the exception type before retrying? 401, 403, 422, 404 must propagate immediately.
stop_reason is checked on every non-streaming response.
check. Truncated responses (stop_reason='max_tokens') must be logged as a warning.
Content blocks are filtered by type, not accessed by index.
access fails when the first block is a tool_use block. The code must filter by
A circuit breaker wraps Claude calls in high-traffic services.
New Claude integration points in services that already have circuit breakers (check existing service patterns) should be wrapped.
Token usage is logged on every call.
response must emit a structured log event with
. Absence of this is a finding.
Log events are structured (JSON), not plain text.
An OpenTelemetry span wraps every Claude call.
In services that already use OTel tracing, a Claude call without a span is a finding. The span must record stop_reason, model, and token counts.
Calls are attributed with team and feature labels.
labels. Missing attribution makes cost spikes unattributable.
PII is stripped or pseudonymised before being included in prompts.
If the code retrieves user records from a database and includes them in a prompt, check that PII fields (name, email, phone, address, SSN) are either absent or replaced with pseudonyms.
Audit events are emitted for prompt sent and response received.
Customer-facing integrations must call the audit logger with hashed user ID, conversation ID, and content hash — not raw content.
Output is scanned for PII before being returned to the caller.
Any response that will be stored, logged, or forwarded should pass through the output PII scanner. Verify the scanner is wired in.
Raw prompt and response content is not present in log events.
with raw text values. These are findings — log hashes, not content.
The happy path is unit-tested with a mocked SDK client.
The mock must be at the
and asserts the caller retries or propagates correctly must exist.
The max_tokens truncation path is tested.
is returned must verify the caller logs a warning and handles the truncated response correctly.
At least one injection scenario is present in the test suite.
For any integration that injects user data near the system prompt, at least one test must confirm that an injection payload in user input does not alter the model's behaviour.
Tests do not hit the real Anthropic API.
instantiation in test files. Production API calls in the test suite are findings — they are flaky, slow, and cost money.

# Example: minimum test coverage for a Claude integration
import pytest
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock
import anthropic

@pytest.fixture
def mock_client(monkeypatch):
    client = AsyncMock(spec=anthropic.AsyncAnthropic)
    resp = MagicMock()
    resp.content = [MagicMock(type="text", text="summary text")]
    resp.stop_reason = "end_turn"
    resp.model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
    resp.usage = MagicMock(input_tokens=100, output_tokens=40)
    client.messages.create.return_value = resp
    monkeypatch.setattr("myservice.claude_client.get_client", lambda: client)
    return client

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_happy_path(mock_client):
    result = await summarise_document("This is a test document.")
    assert "summary" in result.lower()
    mock_client.messages.create.assert_called_once()

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_rate_limit_raises(mock_client):
    mock_client.messages.create.side_effect = anthropic.RateLimitError(
        "rate limited", response=MagicMock(status_code=429, headers={}), body={}
    )
    with pytest.raises(anthropic.RateLimitError):
        await summarise_document("test")

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_truncation_logged(mock_client, caplog):
    mock_client.messages.create.return_value.stop_reason = "max_tokens"
    await summarise_document("long document")
    assert any("truncated" in r.message.lower() for r in caplog.records)
Paste this template as a PR comment when reviewing a Claude integration. Check off each item and leave a specific comment on any line that fails:

## Claude Integration Review

### SDK Usage
- [ ] AsyncAnthropic used in async context
- [ ] Client is a module-level singleton
- [ ] max_retries + timeout set at creation
- [ ] No api_key= literal in constructor
- [ ] Model ID from ModelConfig, not hardcoded
- [ ] max_tokens is a named constant, not inline integer

### Prompt Safety
- [ ] User input only in messages list or delimited section-5
- [ ] Namespaced delimiters (<acme:...>) for injected context
- [ ] Current safety baseline version included verbatim
- [ ] CI injection test passes (link to CI run)

### Error Handling
- [ ] RateLimitError / 529 handled separately from 4xx
- [ ] 4xx errors (422, 401) not retried
- [ ] stop_reason checked on every non-streaming response
- [ ] Content blocks filtered by type (not accessed by index)

### Observability
- [ ] input_tokens + output_tokens logged on every call
- [ ] Structured (JSON) log events (not plain text)
- [ ] OTel span present if service uses tracing
- [ ] Prometheus counter includes team + feature labels

### Data & Privacy
- [ ] PII stripped/pseudonymised before prompt inclusion
- [ ] Audit events emitted (hashed user_id, content hash)
- [ ] Output PII scanner wired in for stored/forwarded responses
- [ ] No raw prompt/response content in log events

### Tests
- [ ] Happy path covered with SDK-boundary mock
- [ ] RateLimitError path tested
- [ ] max_tokens truncation path tested
- [ ] At least one injection scenario in tests
- [ ] No real API calls in test suite

Not every checklist item requires human eyes. Several SDK usage and observability findings can be caught earlier with static analysis hooks in CI, reducing review time to focus on the items that need human judgment:
Literal model IDs outside the central registry
API calls without an upper-bound token limit
import-linter contract: if anthropic in imports, structlog must also be imported
Files that call Claude without structured logging
Content blocks printed to stdout or written to plaintext logs

# .github/workflows/claude-checklist.yml
name: Claude Integration Static Checks

on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  claude-checks:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Detect hardcoded model IDs
        run: |
          if grep -rn 'claude-[0-9]' src/ --include='*.py' | grep -v 'MODEL_ID\|registry\|# pinned'; then
            echo "Hardcoded model ID found outside registry. Use config.MODEL_ID instead."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Detect hardcoded API keys
        run: |
          if grep -rn 'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.*=.*["'"'"'][a-z]' src/ --include='*.py'; then
            echo "Hardcoded API key found. Use environment variables."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Check max_tokens presence
        run: python3 scripts/check-max-tokens.py src/

      - name: Run semgrep rules
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: .semgrep/claude-rules.yml

# .semgrep/claude-rules.yml
rules:
  - id: claude-no-raw-user-input-in-system
    patterns:
      - pattern: |
          client.messages.create(
            ...,
            system=f"...$USER_VAR...",
            ...
          )
    message: "User-controlled variable $USER_VAR interpolated into system prompt.
              Move user data to the messages list, not the system parameter."
    severity: ERROR
    languages: [python]

  - id: claude-missing-stop-reason-check
    patterns:
      - pattern: |
          $RESP = $CLIENT.messages.create(...)
          ...
          $RESP.content[...]
    pattern-not: |
          $RESP = $CLIENT.messages.create(...)
          ...
          $RESP.stop_reason
    message: "Response content accessed without checking stop_reason first.
              A stop_reason of max_tokens means the response is truncated."
    severity: WARNING
    languages: [python]

  - id: claude-bare-except-on-api-call
    patterns:
      - pattern: |
          try:
            $CLIENT.messages.create(...)
          except Exception:
            ...
    message: "Bare Exception catch on Claude API call. Catch specific
              anthropic.RateLimitError / anthropic.APIConnectionError / anthropic.APIStatusError
              to apply the correct retry vs. abort logic."
    severity: WARNING
    languages: [python]
Common Review Findings by Team Maturity
Teams at different stages of Claude adoption tend to produce different failure modes. Calibrate review effort accordingly:
Hardcoded model IDs, missing max_tokens, synchronous client in async code, no retry logic
SDK setup correctness; point to internal bootstrap templates
Familiar with API, new to production ops
No structured logging, token usage not recorded, no circuit breaker, canary skipped
Observability and resilience; point to logging/telemetry standards doc
Monolithic system prompts not under version control, no eval suite, injection tests missing, PII in log fields
Prompt governance and security; point to prompt template governance and review approval docs
Pinned model IDs approaching EOL, quarterly reviews overdue, safety baseline version drift, eval suite not extended for new product features
If an author disputes a finding (e.g., argues a particular retry is intentional), the default resolution path is:
The author adds a code comment explaining the intentional deviation (example: why a particular 4xx is retried in their context).
The reviewer re-reads with the comment and either accepts the explanation or escalates to the prompt lead.
If escalated, the prompt lead makes the final call within one business day.
The outcome is recorded in the PR — either the code changes or the explicit-deviation comment is merged as-is.

Do not merge with an unresolved 'Needs Discussion' review — it signals that the author and reviewer have not reached agreement. An approved review with a waiver comment is acceptable; a dismissed blocking review without resolution is not.

Findings in the Prompt Safety and Data & Privacy dimensions cannot be waived by the author or general reviewer. They require explicit sign-off from a security reviewer. If the security reviewer is unavailable within 48 hours, escalate to the security team lead rather than merging without approval.
Print this table and mark each row during review. Every row must be either Passed, N/A (with justification), or Waived (security reviewer sign-off required for safety/privacy items).
AsyncAnthropic used in async code; no SyncAnthropic in async context
Client constructed once and reused (singleton / dependency injection)
max_tokens set on every API call using a named constant
max_retries passed; not relying on default 2
Model ID from central registry; not hardcoded string
User input isolated in messages list; no f-string interpolation into system
Namespace delimiters used for injected context blocks
Canonical safety baseline pasted verbatim and version comment present
Injection test in eval suite for this endpoint
No retry on 4xx status codes other than 429
Content block type tested before attribute access
structlog JSON log with model_id, feature, team on every call
OTel span wrapping API call with latency and token attributes
Prometheus counter/histogram with feature and team labels
PII stripped or hashed before logging
Audit event emitted for each API call touching personal data
Output content not written to unencrypted plaintext log
Unit tests mock at SDK boundary; no real API calls in CI
Rate-limit path (429) covered by a test
SDK usage, prompt safety, error handling, observability, data/privacy, and tests. A PR with green CI but a missing test for the rate-limit path is not reviewed.
SDK usage findings are mostly mechanical
and automatable: a linter can flag sync clients in async contexts, literal model strings, and missing max_tokens. Consider adding a custom Ruff or mypy plugin for these checks.
Prompt safety findings require human judgement:
no linter can tell whether a variable interpolation introduces an injection risk or is safe server-retrieved data. Reviewers must trace the data flow.
The PR comment template makes the checklist a first-class artifact
of the review, not a mental checklist the reviewer might forget. Each item is either checked or has a specific comment explaining the finding.
Tests are the last gate — and the most often skipped.
Require unit tests for the rate-limit and truncation paths explicitly; these are the paths most likely to cause production incidents and the ones teams most commonly skip.
